target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #39248: Bug: sandbox.mode: "non-main" silently breaks sessions_spawn subagent initialization
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 39248
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/39248
- Title: Bug: sandbox.mode: "non-main" silently breaks sessions_spawn subagent initialization
- State: OPEN
- Author: alexputici
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-live-repro, impact:session-state, impact:crash-loop, issue-rating: 🐚 platinum hermit

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode` is set to `"non-main"`, sub-agents spawned via `sessions_spawn` fail to initialize. Sessions are accepted (return valid `childSessionKey` and `runId`) but never start executing — zero tokens, zero messages, no transcript files created. **No errors appear in gateway logs.**

## Environment

- OpenClaw Version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
- OS: macOS 15.3.0 (Apple Silicon, arm64)
- Node: v25.8.0
- Docker Desktop: running (coding agent sandbox works fine)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "non-main"` in config
2. Restart gateway
3. From main agent, spawn a sub-agent:
   ```
   sessions_spawn(task="Reply with: test", model="sonnet")
   ```
4. Check `sessions_history` or `subagents list` after 30+ seconds

## Expected Behavior

Sub-agent starts within seconds, `systemSent: true`, tokens accumulate, task completes.

## Actual Behavior

- `sessions_spawn` returns `status: "accepted"` with valid session key
- Sub-agent appears in `subagents list` as `running`
- `sessions_history` returns `messages: []` indefinitely
- No transcript file created on disk
- `systemSent` never set to `true`
- `totalTokens` never set
- **Zero error output in gateway logs**
- Eventually times out via `runTimeoutSeconds`

## Bisect Results

Systematically tested with a simple one-line task (`Reply with exactly: ...`):

| Test | sandbox.mode | Docker settings | Result |
|------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| 1 | non-main | network: bridge, readOnlyRoot: true | stall |
| 2 | non-main | network: bridge, readOnlyRoot: false | stall |
| 3 | **off** | (same as test 2) | **works (4s)** |
| 4 | non-main | minimal (image + workdir only) | stall |

The issue is **not** caused by specific Docker settings (network, readOnlyRoot, tmpfs). Any `sandbox.mode` that enables Docker for subagents causes silent failure.

## Notes

- **Cron isolated sessions work fine** with `sandbox.mode: "non-main"` on the same config — they appear to use a different code path that bypasses container initialization.
- **Per-agent sandbox overrides are unaffected**: a coding agent with `sandbox.mode: "all"` and its own Docker settings works correctly.
- Possibly related to #5813 (similar symptoms, different attributed cause).
- The silent failure with zero log output makes this very difficult to diagnose.

## Workaround

Set `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "off"` and use per-agent `sandbox.mode: "all"` overrides for agents that specifically need sandboxing (e.g., coding agents with `tools.exec.host: "sandbox"`).
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #84583: cron announce delivery triggers EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError when user is actively chatting
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 84583
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84583
- Title: cron announce delivery triggers EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError when user is actively chatting
- State: OPEN
- Author: jonah791
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem

When a cron job (sessionTarget="isolated") finishes and its announce delivery sends the result to the same Telegram user, it triggers:

EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released

## Root cause

1. User sends a Telegram message -> session file locked for processing
2. LLM call is made -> embedded prompt lock temporarily released
3. Cron job completes -> announce delivery writes to the same Telegram session (delivery.to = same user ID)
4. Session file changes while the original LLM call is still in flight
5. LLM returns -> tries to resume -> detects file changed -> takeover error

## Relevant config

Cron job delivery:
{
  "sessionTarget": "isolated",
  "delivery": {
    "mode": "announce",
    "channel": "telegram",
    "to": "8790460537"
  }
}

## Expected behavior

Cron announce delivery should not modify the active user session file, or should use a separate write path that doesn't conflict with the embedded agent's prompt lock.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.5.18
- Telegram channel
- Isolated cron jobs with announce delivery

```

- Maintainers can also comment `@clawsweeper review` to request a fresh review only.

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: current main still has a plausible source path where explicit isolated cron announce delivery can append a delivery-mirror message into a resolved per-channel-peer Telegram session, while the embedded runner treats any external transcript change during the released prompt lock as a takeover.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: an isolated cron job with explicit Telegram announce delivery can resolve to a per-channel-peer user session and append a delivery-mirror transcript, while the embedded runner explicitly throws if that session file changes during releaseForPrompt. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.

**Next step**
This is a narrow, source-traceable session concurrency bug in cron delivery mirroring with clear likely files and focused regression coverage.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep the direct channel send, but defer or queue the cron completion awareness for the resolved active destination session instead of immediately appending a delivery-mirror transcript when an isolated cron job targets a chat session that may be in flight.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes, by source inspection: an isolated cron job with explicit Telegram announce delivery can resolve to a per-channel-peer user session and append a delivery-mirror transcript, while the embedded runner explicitly throws if that session file changes during releaseForPrompt. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes, the best fix is likely in the cron delivery mirror/awareness path, not in Telegram sending or by weakening the embedded session takeover guard. The fix should preserve the visible channel send while avoiding immediate transcript mutation of a possibly active destination session.

Label changes:

- add `P2`: The issue is a concrete cron-plus-Telegram session-state bug with a narrow affected workflow rather than a core outage.
- add `impact:message-loss`: The takeover error can abort the user's active in-flight chat response after cron delivery races the same session.
- add `impact:session-state`: The reported failure is caused by concurrent mutation of a session transcript while the embedded runner is preserving session state.
- add `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`: Current issue advisory state selects this label.
- add `clawsweeper:source-repro`: Current issue advisory state selects this label.
- add `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`: Current issue advisory state selects this label.
- add `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`: Current issue advisory state selects this label.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: The issue is a concrete cron-plus-Telegram session-state bug with a narrow affected workflow rather than a core outage.
- `impact:session-state`: The reported failure is caused by concurrent mutation of a session transcript while the embedded runner is preserving session state.
- `impact:message-loss`: The takeover error can abort the user's active in-flight chat response after cron delivery races the same session.

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/session-route.test.ts
- git diff --check

What I checked:

- **Cron delivery mirror path:** dispatchCronDelivery resolves a deliverySessionKey for direct cron delivery and appends a transcript mirror with that sessionKey when the destination is not the awareness main session or the implicit-main bypass case. ([`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts:991`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/48a14e41e233/src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts#L991), [48a14e41e233](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/48a14e41e233))
- **Telegram DM session routing:** Telegram outbound routes are built from the parsed target; direct peers use buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute, which flows through dmScope-aware session-key construction. ([`extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts:521`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/48a14e41e233/extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts#L521), [48a14e41e233](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/48a14e41e233))
- **Per-channel-peer session keys:** With session.dmScope="per-channel-peer", direct messages resolve to agent:<agent>:<channel>:direct:<peer> instead of the main session, so an explicit Telegram DM delivery can target the active user's own session file. ([`src/routing/session-key.ts:204`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/48a14e41e233/src/routing/session-key.ts#L204), [48a14e41e233](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/48a14e41e233))
- **Transcript append writes the session file:** appendAssistantMessageToSessionTranscript delegates to appendExactAssistantMessageToSessionTranscript, resolves the target session file, and appends under the session transcript write lock. - **Takeover condition:** The embedded attempt lock records a transcript fingerprint when releaseForPrompt releases the lock, then throws EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError if the session file fingerprint changes before the post-prompt write lock resumes. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts:288`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/48a14e41e233/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts#L288), [48a14e41e233](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/48a14e41e233))
- **Existing coverage gap:** The cron dispatch tests cover skipping the mirror when the resolved delivery session is the awareness main session, but also preserve mirroring into a resolved per-channel-peer destination session; there is no active-session conflict guard for that path. ([`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts:688`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/48a14e41e233/src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts#L688), [48a14e41e233](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/48a14e41e233))

When the operator later runs `openclaw agents add `, the wizard's "Workspace directory" prompt seeds `initialValue` from `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, agentId)`. For non-default agents that path resolves to `path.join(defaults.workspace, id)` (set by #59789) — i.e. `~/.openclaw/workspace/`, **nested under the main agent's tree**, not the peer-level `~/.openclaw/workspace-` documented in [`docs/concepts/multi-agent.md:57,67`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/concepts/multi-agent.md#L57). If the operator hits Enter, the nested layout becomes the persisted reality: - Skill discovery walking `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/` traverses `/skills/` along with main's, leaking cross-agent skills under OpenClaw's "soft sandbox" model. - `rm -rf ~/.openclaw/workspace` to reset main wipes every nested agent's workspace, memory, and skills. - Recursive memory/grep/glob inside main's workspace silently treats other agents' private files as main's. ## Fix `src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts` — add `suggestPeerAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, id, env)`. The helper only overrides runtime resolution in the **narrow post-setup scenario** where `agents.defaults.workspace` exactly matches `resolveDefaultAgentWorkspaceDir(env)` (the value the setup wizard wrote). In that case it returns the documented peer-level form `~/.openclaw/workspace-`. Every other shape — unset `defaults.workspace`, custom shared roots like `/srv/agents`, `/srv/workspace`, `/srv/workspace-prod`, and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`-isolated multi-gateway setups — defers to `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir` so the contracts from #59789 (shared-base nesting) and per-instance state-dir isolation stay intact. `src/commands/agents.commands.add.ts` — the wizard uses the new helper as `initialValue` only for **brand-new** agents. When `openclaw agents add ` is used to update an existing agent, the prompt still seeds from `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, agentId)` so a pressed-Enter on a routine auth/binding update does not silently relocate the agent. Runtime resolution via `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir` is intentionally unchanged. Existing per-agent `workspace` overrides take precedence, and the #59789 nested layout is preserved for everyone who explicitly configured it. ## Verification - `node` standalone trace through 8 representative configs (default install, OPENCLAW_PROFILE, OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR isolation, /srv/agents shared root, /srv/workspace and /srv/workspace-prod ambiguous bases, /shared-ws #59789, id normalization) — all behave as expected. - Added 6 unit tests in `agent-scope.test.ts` covering each scenario above and 2 wizard tests in `agents.add.test.ts` covering both new-agent prefill and existing-agent update paths. - `oxfmt --check` and `npx oxlint` clean on changed files. - `pnpm tsgo:core` produces the same set of pre-existing unrelated errors as `c070509b7f` (verified by stashing and re-running on clean main). - 4 review iterations with `codex review --base origin/main` against this diff. Final pass: "I did not identify any blocking correctness issues in the diff." ## Test plan - [x] Unit tests added for the new helper covering all 6 documented config shapes. - [x] Wizard tests for both new-agent and existing-agent update paths. - [x] Codex review clean. - [ ] Manual `pnpm openclaw agents add testbug` on a fresh `~/.openclaw` to confirm the prompt prefill matches expectations. - [ ] Manual `pnpm openclaw agents add ` on a config with a customized agent workspace to confirm the prefill preserves it. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-26T04:05:30Z:

- State: OPEN
- Author: stevenepalmer
- Labels: docs, agents, size: M, extensions: tts-local-cli, plugin: azure-speech, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: 🦐 gold shrimp, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: ⏳ waiting on author

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

For agents whose conclusive answer arrives at the end of a long transcript (the common case), the final emit will be stale preamble, not the actual answer. Path: src/agents/harness/selection.ts Line: 118-122 Comment: **Diagnostic records "pi selected" on paths that throw, not select** Both `fallback === "none"` error paths call `recordSelection("pi", "…;fallback-disabled")` immediately before throwing. The diagnostic ring therefore shows `selected=pi fallback=true` for runs that never actually executed on PI — they threw instead. An operator reading `/acp doctor` could incorrectly conclude the request fell back and ran on PI, when in fact it failed outright. Consider recording a distinct status (e.g. `selectedHarnessId: "(none — threw)"`) or skipping the diagnostic entry on the throw path. How can I resolve this? - Author: cedar202510-dotcom
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
# [Feature]: Support native web_search passthrough for ZAI (GLM) and Google (Gemini) providers

### Summary

### Feature Description

OpenClaw already supports native `web_search` tool passthrough for xAI Grok via the Responses API (see `runGrokSearch` in the source code, which sends `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`). 

**Request:** Please add the same native web search passthrough support for **ZAI (Zhipu GLM)** and **Google Gemini** providers, both of which offer free, built-in web search tools in their APIs.

### Why This Matters

Currently, the only way to enable web search in OpenClaw is through the `tools.web.search` configuration, which requires a third-party search API key (Brave, Perplexity, or Grok). However:

- **ZAI/GLM** natively supports `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]` in the API request — it's free and included with the model API
- **Google Gemini** natively supports `tools: [{ google_search: {} }]` — also free

Both of these providers are already built-in to OpenClaw, so users already have valid API keys configured. There's no reason to require a separate paid search API when the model provider offers search for free.

### Current Behavior

When using GLM-5 (zai provider), the agent tells users "I cannot search the web" because OpenClaw doesn't include the native `web_search` tool in the API request body.

### Expected Behavior

When the model provider is `zai` and the user hasn't configured a separate web search provider, OpenClaw should automatically include `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]` in the API request to leverage GLM's native search capability. Similarly for Gemini with `google_search`.

### Reference

- ZAI `web_search` API docs: https://open.bigmodel.cn/dev/api/search/web-search-pro
- Gemini Google Search grounding: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/grounding
- Existing Grok implementation: `runGrokSearch()` in `reply-*.js` already does `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`
- Related PRs: #10454 (Zhipu web search plugin, closed), #13814 (ZAI Search provider, open)

### Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.2.15
- Provider: zai (GLM-5), google (Gemini 2.5 Flash/Pro)

### Problem to solve

OpenClaw 在调用智谱 GLM 和 Google Gemini 模型时，没有传递这些模型 API 原生自带的免费搜索工具，导致 agent 无法联网搜索。

具体表现：使用 GLM-5 作为默认模型时，agent 回复"我没有办法进行搜索"。但智谱 API 原生支持在请求中加入 `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]` 即可免费使用搜索，无需额外 API Key。

目前 OpenClaw 已经对 xAI Grok 做了原生 web_search 透传（见源码 `runGrokSearch()` 中的 `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`），但没有对 ZAI（GLM）和 Google（Gemini）做同样的支持。

当前要使用搜索功能只能配置 Brave/Perplexity/Grok 等付费第三方搜索 API，而 GLM 和 Gemini 自带的免费搜索被浪费了。

### Proposed solution

参考已有的 Grok 实现（`runGrokSearch()`），为 ZAI 和 Google provider 添加原生搜索工具透传：

1. **ZAI (GLM)**：当 provider 为 `zai` 时，在 API 请求 body 中自动加入 `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`
   - 文档：https://open.bigmodel.cn/dev/api/search/web-search-pro
   - 免费，无需额外 API Key

2. **Google (Gemini)**：当 provider 为 `google` 时，在请求中加入 `tools: [{ google_search: {} }]`
   - 文档：https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/grounding
   - 免费，无需额外 API Key

可以通过配置项控制是否启用（如 `agents.defaults.nativeSearch: true`），在用户未配置第三方搜索 provider 时自动使用模型原生搜索作为 fallback。

相关 PR 参考：#10454、#13814

### Alternatives considered

_No response_

### Impact

受影响的用户/系统/渠道：
所有使用 ZAI（智谱 GLM）和 Google（Gemini）作为默认模型的 OpenClaw 用户。这两个 provider 是 OpenClaw 内置支持且推荐的免费/低成本模型。

严重程度：阻碍工作流程
Agent 在需要实时信息时（如查天气、新闻、技术文档、价格等）完全无法响应，只能回复"我无法搜索"。用户被迫手动搜索后粘贴给 agent，失去了 AI 助手的核心价值。

频率：总是
只要用户的模型是 GLM 或 Gemini，且没有额外配置付费第三方搜索 API（Brave/Perplexity），搜索功能就完全不可用。这不是偶发问题，而是 100% 复现。

后果：
- 用户体验严重下降：agent 无法回答任何需要实时数据的问题
- 额外的经济成本：用户被迫购买 Brave/Perplexity 等第三方搜索 API Key，而模型本身已经提供了免费搜索
- 不一致的体验：同样的 GLM-5 模型在智谱官网能搜索，在 OpenClaw 里却不能，造成用户困惑
- 已有先例未推广：OpenClaw 已为 Grok 实现了原生搜索透传，但未扩展到同样支持原生搜索的 GLM 和 Gemini

### Evidence/examples

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When using omni-modal models (e.g. `mimo-v2-omni`, Gemini 3 Flash) that natively support audio input, OpenClaw should be able to send voice notes directly to the model instead of routing them through Whisper/transcription first.

## Current Behavior

- User sends a voice note (WhatsApp/Telegram/etc.)
- OpenClaw downloads the audio file
- Audio is sent to a transcription provider (Whisper, Deepgram, OpenAI transcribe)
- Transcript text is injected into the agent context
- **Result:** Tone, emotion, prosody, and multi-speaker separation are lost

## Desired Behavior

Add a configuration option (e.g. `tools.media.audio.native: true`) that, when enabled:

1. Detects the audio MIME type
2. Sends the audio data directly to the configured omni-modal model as a native media part (same way images are handled)
3. The model processes the audio natively — preserving tone, emotion, and speaker diarization

## Why This Matters

- **Latency:** No intermediate STT step = faster response
- **Richness:** Preserves emotional context, tone, prosody that transcription discards
- **Multi-speaker:** Native omni-modal models can separate speakers; Whisper cannot
- **Model capabilities:** Models like mimo-v2-omni and Gemini 3 Flash are designed for native audio — this feature unlocks their full potential

## Use Case

Running mimo-v2-omni as the primary model via OpenRouter. The model supports native audio with speaker diarization, but OpenClaw currently forces all voice notes through Whisper, wasting the model omni-modal capabilities.

## Proposed Config

```json5
{
  tools: {
    media: {
      audio: {
        native: true, // Send audio directly to omni-modal model instead of transcribing
        fallbackToTranscription: true // Fall back to Whisper if model does not support audio
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Related

- Original issue: #1732 (closed as stale)

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.3.11
- Model: `openrouter/xiaomi/mimo-v2-omni`
- Channel: WhatsApp
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- Ryce at 2026-03-21T15:19:56Z:
This is a great feature request — omni-modal models that process audio natively bypass the STT transcription step entirely, which creates a verification and continuity gap.

**The core problem with bypassing STT:**

When OpenClaw uses STT to transcribe audio, the transcript becomes part of the session transcript — it is a durable, searchable record of what was said. When the model processes audio directly, the session transcript shows only the model's *interpretation* of the audio, not the audio itself or any transcription.

This means:

1. **No verifiable record of what was heard** — if the model misinterprets an accent, mumbles, or background noise, there is no transcript to audit against
2. **Session continuity breaks on context loss** — if the session is reset or restored from snapshot, the "heard" audio is gone; only the model's summary remains
3. **Audit trail is one step removed from ground truth** — the session transcript says "user said X" but X is already the model's interpretation, not the original

**Where KeepMyClaw fits:**

KeepMyClaw snapshots capture the complete session context at defined intervals. When an omni-modal model processes audio natively, the snapshot should record:

- The audio blob itself (or a hash + reference) so the original is verifiable
- The model's transcription/interpretation so the delta between heard and interpreted is auditable
- The full audio context (surrounding messages, speaker identity) for replay

Without this, omni-modal audio processing trades transcript verifiability for speed — useful in the happy path, dangerous when the session needs to be audited or restored.

**A practical addition:**

Even without full audio archival, a "last-audio-transcript-delta" field in the snapshot would surface cases where the model's interpretation significantly diverged from what a human would expect — useful for catching hallucinated transcriptions of accented speech, technical terms, or noisy environments.

https://keepmyclaw.com

- jojo2a at 2026-03-23T12:55:35Z:
Thanks for the thoughtful analysis, Ryce. The audit trail concern is valid — but I think there's a pragmatic middle ground.

My primary use case: **voice messaging via WhatsApp/Telegram**. Users send voice notes expecting the assistant to *hear* them, not to read a butchered transcription. With Whisper (even large-v3), multi-speaker accents, background noise, and emotional tone are systematically lost. The model working with native audio produces dramatically better responses — even if the transcript is an interpretation.

**Pragmatic solution:**

1. Add a config flag `tools.media.audio.native: true` (opt-in, not default)
2. When enabled, send raw audio to omni-modal models (mimo-v2-omni, Gemini 3.x Flash, etc.) — skip STT entirely
3. Log a minimal metadata entry in the session: `{"audio": "<hash>", "duration": "12s", "model_interpretation": "..."}` — enough for audit without storing full blobs
4. Fallback to STT if the model doesn't support audio input

This is **not** about replacing STT for all cases. It's about giving users a choice: fast & rich (native audio) vs. verifiable & searchable (STT transcript).

**Why now:** Omni-modal models are becoming the default. Gemini 3.x Flash, MiMo-V2-Omni, and the upcoming GPT-5.4 all process audio natively. OpenClaw should support this natively instead of forcing every audio through Whisper as a bottleneck.

Happy to test with my setup (mimo-v2-omni on TrueNAS) and provide feedback.

Body:
```markdown
## Severity Assessment

### CVSS Assessment

| Metric | v3.1 | v4.0 |
|--------|------|------|
| **Score** | 7.6 / 10.0 | 7.2 / 10.0 |
| **Severity** | High | High |
| **Vector** | CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:H/A:L | CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:L/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N |
| **Calculator** | [CVSS v3.1 Calculator](https://www.first.org/cvss/calculator/3.1#CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:H/A:L) | [CVSS v4.0 Calculator](https://www.first.org/cvss/calculator/4.0#CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:L/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N) |

### Threat Model Alignment
**Classification:** `security-specific`

This finding crosses the `configWrites` authorization boundary, which is OpenClaw's documented control for restricting which channels may make persistent configuration changes. The `configWrites=false` setting is an explicit operator-configured trust boundary: it prevents a given channel from initiating writes that modify access control state. The `/allowlist --store` path bypasses this boundary by skipping `resolveConfigWriteDeniedText` entirely when `parsed.target === "store"`, allowing a sender on a write-restricted channel to directly mutate the pairing allowlist store — a security-relevant credential file under `~/.openclaw/credentials/`. This is not out of scope: it is not a multi-tenant isolation claim, not a prompt-injection-only path, and not a trusted-operator-initiated action; the attacker is an already-command-authorized sender, not the gateway operator, and the bypass defeats a control the operator deliberately configured to restrict writes from that channel.
## Impact
An authorized sender on a channel where `configWrites=false` can invoke `/allowlist add|remove --store <id>` to add or remove entries from the persistent pairing allowlist store, defeating the operator's intent to block channel-initiated access-control writes. Because pairing-store entries are merged into the effective DM `allowFrom` list at authorization time, this bypass grants or revokes DM access for future senders without the operator's consent.

## Affected Component
**File:** `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-allowlist.ts:495-496,498-550,672-684`
```typescript
// Line 495-496: shouldUpdateConfig is false when --store is passed
const shouldUpdateConfig = parsed.target !== "store";
const shouldTouchStore = parsed.target !== "config" && listPairingChannels().includes(channelId);

// Lines 498-670: configWrites gate only runs when shouldUpdateConfig is true
if (shouldUpdateConfig) {
  // ...
  const deniedText = resolveConfigWriteDeniedText({
    cfg: params.cfg,
    channel: params.command.channel,
    channelId,
    accountId: params.ctx.AccountId,
    gatewayClientScopes: params.ctx.GatewayClientScopes,
    target: editSpec.writeTarget,
  });
  if (deniedText) {
    return { shouldContinue: false, reply: { text: deniedText } };
  }
  // ... config file mutation branch ...
}

// Lines 672-684: --store path reaches here with no configWrites check
if (!shouldTouchStore) {
  return { shouldContinue: false, reply: { text: "⚠️ This channel does not support allowlist storage." } };
}
await updatePairingStoreAllowlist({
  action: parsed.action,
  channelId,
  accountId,
  entry: parsed.entry,
});
```

## Technical Reproduction
1. Configure a pairing-capable channel (e.g. Telegram or WhatsApp) with `commands.text=true`, `commands.config=true`, `channels.<provider>.dmPolicy="pairing"`, and `channels.<provider>.configWrites=false`.
2. Ensure at least one sender is already command-authorized on that channel (via existing allowlist or pairing approval).
3. Send `/allowlist add dm --store <target-id>` from that authorized sender.
4. The handler sets `shouldUpdateConfig = false` because `parsed.target === "store"`, bypasses the entire `if (shouldUpdateConfig)` block including the `resolveConfigWriteDeniedText` call, and falls through to `updatePairingStoreAllowlist(...)` at line 679, returning `✅ DM allowlist added: pairing store.`
5. Confirm the entry persists in `~/.openclaw/credentials/<channel>-allowFrom.json` (or the account-scoped variant).
6. Send a DM from the newly enrolled `<target-id>`. `readChannelAllowFromStore(...)` returns the entry, `mergeDmAllowFromSources` merges it into `effectiveAllowFrom` (`src/security/dm-policy-shared.ts:44-50`), and the sender is treated as authorized for future DM commands.

## Demonstrated Impact
The root cause is in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-allowlist.ts`: the `configWrites` authorization gate is gated inside the `if (shouldUpdateConfig)` branch (line 498), which is only entered when the user targets the config file. When the user passes `--store`, `shouldUpdateConfig` is `false` and the entire gate — including `resolveConfigWriteDeniedText` — is never evaluated. The code unconditionally reaches `updatePairingStoreAllowlist` at lines 679-684.

The store mutation is security-relevant. `readChannelAllowFromStore` (`src/pairing/pairing-store.ts:542-563`) returns persisted entries, which `mergeDmAllowFromSources` and `resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists` (`src/security/dm-policy-shared.ts:44-50`) merge into the effective DM allowlist. `resolveSenderCommandAuthorization` (`src/plugin-sdk/command-auth.ts:72-105`) then uses these merged lists for access decisions, meaning a new sender enrolled via the bypassed store mutation will be authorized for future DM commands.

The docs at `docs/tools/slash-commands.md:127-128` explicitly state that `/allowlist add|remove` "honors channel `configWrites`," but the `--store` path violates that contract. An operator who set `configWrites=false` to prevent a shared or automated channel from making persistent access-control changes gets no protection when the attacker uses the `--store` flag.

## Environment
Verified against `openclaw/openclaw` release `v2026.4.24` (commit `e672b61417af5c45b0431df6d9109a1f4b618ef5`, published `2026-04-25T18:15:17Z`).

Preconditions:
- A pairing-capable channel (Telegram, WhatsApp, iMessage, Discord, or any channel returned by `listPairingChannels()`)
- `commands.config=true` on that channel
- `channels.<provider>.configWrites=false`
- At least one already command-authorized sender on that channel

## Remediation Advice
Apply the same `configWrites` authorization check to store-targeted `/allowlist` mutations as to config-targeted ones by calling `resolveConfigWriteDeniedText` before reaching `updatePairingStoreAllowlist`, regardless of the `parsed.target` value. If pairing-store edits are intentionally meant to bypass `configWrites`, document a distinct permission key for that surface and update the slash-commands docs to reflect the separate boundary rather than advertising a single unified `configWrites` gate.

<!-- submission-marker:AA-lly-allowlist-store-bypasses-configwrites -->

```

github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 66467
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66467
- Title: ACP session/update usage_update notification fails validation when used=null (Invalid params / expected object, received undefined)
- State: OPEN
- Author: fisher1006
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Discord-bound ACP sessions can fail during `session/update` notification handling when the ACP runtime emits a `usage_update` notification with `used: null`.

In the observed failure, OpenClaw logs:

```text
Error handling notification {
  jsonrpc: '2.0',
  method: 'session/update',
  params: {
    sessionId: 'ba706432-d8f3-431b-ad57-bd0f0a5dc131',
    update: {
      sessionUpdate: 'usage_update',
      used: null,
      size: 200000,
      cost: [Object]
    }
  }
} {
  code: -32602,
  message: 'Invalid params'
}
```

And the validation payload includes repeated schema errors such as:

```text
Invalid input: expected object, received undefined
sessionUpdate: { _errors: [Array] }
title: { _errors: [Array] }
toolCallId: { _errors: [Array] }
entries: { _errors: [Array] }
availableCommands: { _errors: [Array] }
currentModeId: { _errors: [Array] }
configOptions: { _errors: [Array] }
used: { _errors: [Array] }
```

This appears to be a runtime/schema mismatch in OpenClaw's ACP notification handling, not a generic "session missing" issue.

## Why this seems distinct from existing ACP/Discord issues

I checked existing issues around:

- Discord-bound ACP persistent resume failures / binding restore
- generic `ACP_TURN_FAILED`
- Discord slash/interaction `40060 Interaction has already been acknowledged`

Those are related symptoms in the same area, but this repro is narrower:

- the ACP session **does exist**
- the runtime **does emit** `session/update`
- the failure happens when OpenClaw handles a specific `usage_update` payload
- the bad payload shape includes `used: null`

So this looks like a notification schema/validator bug, not just a Discord binding problem.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: local main install on macOS arm64
- OS: macOS Darwin 25.x (Apple Silicon)
- Channel: Discord
- ACP backend: bundled `acpx`
- Runtime context: Discord-bound ACP thread/session path

## Evidence

### 1) ACP sessions are being created

`openclaw status` showed Discord ACP binding sessions being created successfully, so this is not a total runtime outage and not simply "session was never created".

### 2) Runtime code suggests `usage_update` should be accepted

In bundled ACPX runtime code:

`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/acpx/node_modules/acpx/dist/runtime.js`

there is explicit handling for `usage_update`:

```js
case "usage_update": {
  const used = asOptionalFiniteNumber(payload.used);
  const size = asOptionalFiniteNumber(payload.size);
  return {
    type: "status",
    text: used != null && size != null ? `usage updated: ${used}/${size}` : "usage updated",
    tag: "usage_update",
    ...used != null ? { used } : {},
    ...size != null ? { size } : {}
  };
}
```

This implies `usage_update` is a known notification type and `used` may be absent/non-finite without being fatal at the runtime projection layer.

### 3) But gateway notification handling rejects the incoming notification

From `gateway.err.log` on 2026-04-14:

```text
2026-04-14T11:46:11.775+08:00 Error handling notification {
  jsonrpc: '2.0',
  method: 'session/update',
  params: {
    sessionId: 'ba706432-d8f3-431b-ad57-bd0f0a5dc131',
    update: {
      sessionUpdate: 'usage_update',
      used: null,
      size: 200000,
      cost: [Object]
    }
  }
} {
  code: -32602,
  message: 'Invalid params',
  data: {
    _errors: [
      'Invalid input: expected object, received undefined',
      ...
    ],
    sessionUpdate: { _errors: [Array] },
    title: { _errors: [Array] },
    toolCallId: { _errors: [Array] },
    entries: { _errors: [Array] },
    availableCommands: { _errors: [Array] },
    currentModeId: { _errors: [Array] },
    configOptions: { _errors: [Array] },
    used: { _errors: [Array] }
  }
}
```

The same error repeated several times for the same session id.

## Suspected root cause

The likely root cause is that OpenClaw validates `session/update` notifications against an overly strict or wrong schema shape for ACP session updates.

More specifically, one of these is probably true:

1. The gateway expects `used` to be an object/number in a stricter union, but ACPX can emit `used: null`.
2. The gateway is validating a `usage_update` payload against a union/schema intended for multiple update variants, and missing optional fields are being treated as required object fields.
3. There is a mismatch between the `session/update` envelope shape emitted by bundled `acpx` and the schema expected by the OpenClaw ACP notification handler.

The repeated `_errors` for unrelated fields (`title`, `toolCallId`, `entries`, `availableCommands`, `currentModeId`, `configOptions`) suggest a broken discriminated-union parse or wrong schema selection, not just one bad field.

## Related symptom (probably secondary, not root cause)

In the same investigation window, Discord also logged:

```text
DiscordError: Interaction has already been acknowledged.
discordCode: 40060
```

and slow `INTERACTION_CREATE` listener warnings.

That looks like a downstream delivery/interaction symptom, but not the root cause of the ACP notification parse failure above.

## Expected behavior

OpenClaw should accept ACP `session/update` notifications for `usage_update` when fields like `used` are `null` or omitted, or at minimum ignore non-critical usage fields instead of failing the whole notification.

## Actual behavior

OpenClaw rejects the notification with:

```text
code: -32602
message: Invalid params
```

and the ACP turn/path becomes unstable in Discord-bound sessions.

## What would help

- Identify the exact schema/parser used for `session/update` notifications in the gateway ACP integration.
- Confirm whether `usage_update.used = null` is intended/allowed from bundled `acpx`.
- Relax or correct the schema so optional/variant fields do not make the entire notification invalid.
- If this is a bundled version skew between OpenClaw and ACPX, document the compatible versions or pin the bundled pair.

```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #71487: Web UI: add a clear TTS toggle and default voice picker in Settings
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 71487
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/71487
- Title: Web UI: add a clear TTS toggle and default voice picker in Settings
- State: OPEN
- Author: wen0531
- Labels: P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem
Right now TTS is configurable, but the UX is much more command/config driven than it should be.

In practice, a user can enable TTS with commands like:
- `openclaw infer tts enable`
- `openclaw infer tts disable`
- `openclaw infer tts set-provider --provider microsoft`

and preferences are written to `~/.openclaw/settings/tts.json`.

That works technically, but from the Control UI / webchat perspective, there is no obvious visible "switch" for:
- turning TTS on/off
- seeing whether it is currently enabled
- selecting a default provider
- selecting a default voice

This creates a mismatch between capability and discoverability.

## Request
Please add a first-class TTS section in the Control UI Settings (or another obvious UI surface) with:

1. **A visible TTS enable/disable toggle**
   - reflect current state
   - persist to the same underlying preferences

2. **Default provider selector**
   - show available/configured providers
   - indicate unavailable/unconfigured providers clearly

3. **Default voice selector**
   - filter by selected provider
   - especially important for providers like Microsoft/Google/MiniMax with many voices

4. **Optional test/play button**
   - generate a short sample line with the currently selected provider/voice
   - helps users verify the setup without using CLI commands

## Why this matters
TTS already exists and can be configured, but users reasonably expect an actual UI switch for a feature this visible. Right now the product feels like it has TTS "under the hood" rather than as a polished surfaced capability.

## Concrete example
A user wanted to:
- enable TTS
- set default voice to `zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`

This ended up being done through CLI/config instead of a simple Settings toggle + voice dropdown.

## Suggested UX copy
- **Text-to-Speech**
- Toggle: "Read replies aloud when supported"
- Provider: dropdown
- Voice: dropdown
- Test button: "Play sample"

Thanks — this feels like a pretty high-leverage polish fix.

```

Greptile Summary

This PR adds an optional `encodingFormat` config field to `memorySearch.remote`, allowing users to specify `"float"` or `"base64"` when making embedding requests to OpenAI-compatible APIs. The schema, type, and request-body changes are clean and backward compatible — however, there is a critical gap in the response handling path. - **Schema** (`zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`): Correctly validates `"float"` | `"base64"` as an optional field. - **Types** (`embeddings.ts`): `encodingFormat` properly added to the `remote` options type. - **Provider** (`embeddings-remote-provider.ts`): Correctly conditionally adds `encoding_format` to the outgoing request body. - **Missing decode path** (`embeddings-remote-fetch.ts`, unchanged): When `encoding_format: "base64"` is used, OpenAI-compatible APIs return each embedding as a Base64-encoded `float32` byte string — not a JSON `number[]`. The existing `parse` callback in `fetchRemoteEmbeddingVectors` unconditionally casts `entry.embedding` as `number[]`, so base64 strings will pass through silently, producing corrupted or empty embedding vectors for all memory searches. The decode step (Base64 → `Buffer` → `Float32Array` → `number[]`) was not added.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- Not safe to merge — the `base64` encoding option is exposed in config but the response is never decoded, silently corrupting all embeddings when used. - The config, type, and request changes are correct in isolation, but there is a critical missing piece: the response parsing layer in `embeddings-remote-fetch.ts` always treats `entry.embedding` as `number[]`. When `encoding_format: "base64"` is requested, the API returns a base64 string for each embedding — not a float array. This string passes through the unchecked TypeScript cast and reaches downstream vector-similarity code as corrupt data. The `"float"` default path is unaffected, so existing behavior is safe, but the newly advertised `"base64"` option would silently break memory search for any user who enables it. - `src/memory/embeddings-remote-fetch.ts` — the `parse` callback needs a branch to Base64-decode the embedding string when `encoding_format: "base64"` was used.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/memory/embeddings-remote-provider.ts Line: 32-34 Comment: **Base64 response not decoded — embeddings will be silently broken** When `encoding_format: "base64"` is sent in the request, OpenAI-compatible APIs return each `embedding` field as a Base64-encoded string of raw IEEE 754 little-endian `float32` bytes — not a JSON `number[]`. The response parser in `embeddings-remote-fetch.ts` always treats `entry.embedding` as `number[]`: ```ts // embeddings-remote-fetch.ts (unchanged) const typedPayload = payload as { data?: Array<{ embedding?: number[] }>; }; return data.map((entry) => entry.embedding ?? []); ``` When the format is `"base64"`, `entry.embedding` will actually be a string (e.g., `"ABCDef...=="`). The TypeScript cast silently lies, and the downstream consumers receive a `string` where they expect `number[]`. This will corrupt all memory searches that rely on vector similarity — the vectors will either be empty (if typed checks reject the string) or be nonsense values. To make `"base64"` actually work, the fetch/parse layer needs to detect the format and decode the Base64 string to a `Float32Array`. For example: ```ts function decodeBase64Embedding(b64: string): number[] { const binary = Buffer.from(b64, "base64"); const floats = new Float32Array(binary.buffer, binary.byteOffset, binary.byteLength / 4); return Array.from(floats); } ``` And the `parse` callback (or a wrapper around `fetchRemoteEmbeddingVectors`) would need to branch on whether `encoding_format` was `"base64"`. Until this decoding is in place, configuring `encodingFormat: "base64"` will produce silently incorrect embeddings. Consider either adding the decode path before merging, or preventing `"base64"` from being a valid config value until the full round-trip is supported. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Today the shared `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText` already turns these empty-turn shapes into a user-visible \"⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response\" payload, but no test in this file exercises that path on a non-GPT-5 provider, so a future tightening of the strict-agentic guard or the reasoning-only detector could regress it without CI catching the break. ### The shapes Both shapes have been reported against NemoClaw as \"TUI spinner hangs indefinitely, no response\": - NVIDIA/NemoClaw#2099 — TUI hangs 8+ minutes, 0 output tokens, spinner stuck on `flibbertigibbeting…` - NVIDIA/NemoClaw#2051 — model ends the turn with only a thinking block, no tool call, no visible text, `stopReason: \"stop\"`, OpenClaw treats it as end-of-turn - NVIDIA/NemoClaw#1193 — `payloads: []`, `stopReason: \"stop\"`, only ~11 output tokens Specifically covered by the new tests: 1. `stopReason=\"stop\"` + a single **unsigned** `thinking` content block. Unsigned thinking hits `assessLastAssistantMessage === \"incomplete-thinking\"` (not `\"incomplete-text\"`), so `isReasoningOnlyAssistantTurn` returns false. The empty-response fallback is what prevents the run from resolving with `payloads: undefined`. 2. `stopReason=\"stop\"` + fully empty `content: []`. The retry instruction resolvers (`resolveEmptyResponseRetryInstruction`, `resolveReasoningOnlyRetryInstruction`) are gated on the strict-agentic supported provider/model set, so the nvidia lane must **not** retry — the tests assert a single `runEmbeddedAttempt` call but still require a terminal `isError: true` payload containing \"Please try again\". ## Why test-only This is purely a regression pin, not a behavior change. The shared guard already handles both shapes correctly on main; this PR just makes that contract explicit for any non-strict-agentic provider that ends turns with \"empty + terminal stopReason\". ## Test plan - [x] \`node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts\` — 53 passed - [x] Filtered to the new cases — both pass: \`... -t \"nemotron\"\` → 2 passed ## AI-assisted - [x] AI-assisted (Claude) - [x] Fully tested locally (vitest on the affected file) - [x] I understand the code: the tests pin existing behavior of `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText` falling through to the `emptyResponseAssistant` branch when the assistant turn has no visible text, no tool activity, and a terminal stopReason, for a provider/model pair outside the strict-agentic supported set. - [x] No production code changed — test-only regression coverage for a previously-reported real-world hang ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-23T15:20:49Z:

- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85048
- Title: Manifest-id mismatch warning fires for ~30+ built-in plugins on gateway start (xai, anthropic, brave, etc.)

- Labels: P3, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
# Manifest-id mismatch warning fires for ~30+ built-in plugins on gateway start (xai, anthropic, brave, etc.)

**Version:** OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (commit a185ca2)

## Summary

On gateway start, OpenClaw emits a `plugin id mismatch` warning for every built-in plugin whose `entry hint` (likely derived from the npm package or directory name) doesn't exactly match the `id` field in its `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest. This appears to be a naming-convention drift across the plugin catalog rather than a per-plugin bug. The plugins load and function normally — warnings are cosmetic — but the gateway logs ~30 of these lines per startup, which is operator log noise.

## Example warning lines

From `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`, 2026-04-01T14:09:59:

```
Config warnings:
- plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock: plugin amazon-bedrock: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "amazon-bedrock", entry hints "amazon-bedrock-provider")
- plugins.entries.anthropic: plugin anthropic: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "anthropic", entry hints "anthropic-provider")
- plugins.entries.brave: plugin brave: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "brave", entry hints "brave-plugin")
- plugins.entries.browser: plugin browser: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "browser", entry hints "browser-plugin")
- plugins.entries.duckduckgo: plugin duckduckgo: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "duckduckgo", entry hints "duckduckgo-plugin")
- plugins.entries.exa: plugin exa: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "exa", entry hints "exa-plugin")
- plugins.entries.firecrawl: plugin firecrawl: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "firecrawl", entry hints "firecrawl-plugin")
- plugins.entries.github-copilot: plugin github-copilot: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "github-copilot", entry hints "github-copilot-provider")
- plugins.entries.google: plugin google: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "google", entry hints "google-plugin")
- plugins.entries.xai: plugin xai: plugin id mismatch (manifest uses "xai", entry hints "xai-plugin")
... [~30 entries total]
```

## Concrete xAI example

- Manifest: `dist/extensions/xai/openclaw.plugin.json` → `"id": "xai"`
- Package: `dist/extensions/xai/package.json` → `"name": "@openclaw/xai-plugin"`
- Entry hint inferred from the package name's basename → `"xai-plugin"` ≠ `"xai"`

## Pattern

The convention appears to be:

- Manifest `id`: short, e.g. `xai`, `anthropic`, `brave`
- Package `name`: `@openclaw/<id>-plugin` or `@openclaw/<id>-provider`
- The entry-hint resolution presumably strips `@openclaw/` and uses the rest as-is, producing the suffixed form

The warning fires because manifest-id and entry-hint don't string-match.

## Suggested resolutions (pick whichever fits direction)

1. Update the manifest-id resolution to normalize-then-compare (strip `-plugin` / `-provider` suffixes before equality check).
2. Update manifest `id` to match package basename (`xai-plugin`, `anthropic-provider`, etc.) — wider change, touches `id` semantics across the catalog.
3. Downgrade this specific warning to `debug` level and only emit at startup when the operator opts into verbose plugin diagnostics.

## Impact

None functional — all plugins load and run. Just operator log noise on gateway start.

## Repro

1. Install `openclaw@2026.5.19` (or any recent version)
2. Start the gateway (`openclaw gateway run`)
3. Tail `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` and grep for `plugin id mismatch`

Labels: P3, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster
```
title: Manifest-id mismatch warning fires for ~30+ built-in plugins on gateway start (xai, anthropic, brave, etc.)

- State: OPEN
- Author: GunnarHelliesen
- Labels: bug, P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
# [Bug]: `image` tool fails for Bedrock with `auth mode: aws-sdk` — `requireApiKey` throws even when AWS SDK creds are available

### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

The `image` tool (media-understanding image-description path) always requires an explicit `apiKey` when resolving provider auth. For `amazon-bedrock` using IAM role / instance profile / `AWS_PROFILE` credentials (i.e. `ResolvedProviderAuth.mode === "aws-sdk"`), no static API key exists — the AWS SDK resolves credentials at call time. The chat/completion path handles this correctly via an `allowMissingApiKeyModes` escape hatch; the image path does not, and throws before the Bedrock call is ever made.

Net effect: every image analysis against a Bedrock model fails, even when chat against the same model works fine.

Affected models tested (all fail identically)

- `amazon-bedrock/us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7`
- `amazon-bedrock/us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `amazon-bedrock/us.amazon.nova-pro-v1:0`
- `amazon-bedrock/stability.stable-image-ultra-v1:1` (same provider, same failure)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure a Bedrock-backed model as the image model, with credentials provided via AWS SDK chain (IAM role, instance profile, or `AWS_PROFILE`) — **no** static `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` or API-key-style credential.
2. Confirm chat works (`openclaw chat ...` with a Bedrock model returns text).
3. Invoke the `image` tool from any agent, e.g.:

   ```
   image(image="/path/to/test.png", model="sonnet", prompt="What is in this image?")
   ```

### Expected behavior

Image tool succeeds using AWS SDK credential resolution, same as chat.

### Actual behavior

All image requests to Bedrock fail with:

```
All image models failed (2): amazon-bedrock/us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6:
  No API key resolved for provider "amazon-bedrock" (auth mode: aws-sdk).
  | amazon-bedrock/us.amazon.nova-pro-v1:0:
  No API key resolved for provider "amazon-bedrock" (auth mode: aws-sdk).
```

Tried models: opus, sonnet, nova — all fail with the same error. Chat with the same models on the same agent works.

Root Cause:

In `src/media-understanding/image.ts`, `resolveImageRuntime` unconditionally calls `requireApiKey`:

```ts
// src/media-understanding/image.ts (around L197-L206)
const apiKeyInfo = await getApiKeyForModel({ model, ... });
const apiKey = requireApiKey(apiKeyInfo, model.provider);   // throws for aws-sdk
authStorage.setRuntimeApiKey(model.provider, apiKey);
return { apiKey, model };
```

`requireApiKey` (in `src/agents/model-auth-runtime-shared.ts`) throws whenever `auth.apiKey` is empty, regardless of `auth.mode`:

```ts
export function requireApiKey(auth: ResolvedProviderAuth, provider: string): string {
  const key = normalizeSecretInput(auth.apiKey);
  if (key) return key;
  throw new Error(`No API key resolved for provider "${provider}" (auth mode: ${auth.mode}).`);
}
```

The chat path (`prepareSimpleCompletionModel` in `src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.ts`) has an explicit `allowMissingApiKeyModes` parameter for exactly this case:

```ts
// src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.ts (around L182-L194)
if (
  !rawApiKey &&
  !hasMissingApiKeyAllowance({ mode: auth.mode, allowMissingApiKeyModes: params.allowMissingApiKeyModes })
) {
  return { error: `No API key resolved for provider "${resolved.model.provider}" (auth mode: ${auth.mode}).`, auth };
}
```

The image tool has no equivalent escape hatch. Same pattern affects:

- `src/media-understanding/image.ts:205` — `resolveImageRuntime` (primary image path)
- `src/media-understanding/image.ts:307` — `resolveMinimaxVlmFallbackRuntime` (MiniMax VLM fallback)
- `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:407` — `primaryApiKey: requireApiKey(auth, params.providerId)` (audio/video transcription path; same bug class, will fail for Bedrock Whisper/transcription if used)

Proposed fix:

Give the media-understanding resolvers the same `allowMissingApiKeyModes` treatment as `prepareSimpleCompletionModel`, default `["aws-sdk"]` when the provider is `amazon-bedrock`, and pass the empty `apiKey` through to `complete()` — which already resolves AWS SDK credentials at call time for Bedrock (that's why chat works).

Rough shape:

```ts
// src/media-understanding/image.ts :: resolveImageRuntime
const apiKeyInfo = await getApiKeyForModel({ model, ... });
const rawApiKey = apiKeyInfo.apiKey?.trim();
if (!rawApiKey && apiKeyInfo.mode !== "aws-sdk") {
  requireApiKey(apiKeyInfo, model.provider); // preserves existing error for non-aws-sdk
}
const apiKey = rawApiKey ?? "";
if (apiKey) authStorage.setRuntimeApiKey(model.provider, apiKey);
return { apiKey, model };
```

Apply the same pattern to `resolveMinimaxVlmFallbackRuntime` and `runner.entries.ts:407`.

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw 2026.4.24 (cbcfdf6)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS / aarch64 on AWS EC2

### Install method

npm global

### Model

amazon-bedrock/us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> AWS Bedrock SDK default chain (IAM instance profile) -> opus 4.7

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

Labels: bug, P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster
```
title: [Bug]: `image` tool fails for Bedrock with `auth mode: aws-sdk` — `requireApiKey` throws even when AWS SDK creds are available

github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 86868
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86868
- Title: Embedded runtime: model fallback chain breaks at intermediate candidates instead of walking to the last entry
- State: OPEN
- Author: John1Tang
- Labels: (none)

Body:
```markdown
# Embedded runtime: model fallback chain breaks at intermediate candidates instead of walking to the last entry

## Summary

In embedded runtime mode, when a model in the middle of the `fallbacks` array fails, the fallback chain does **not** continue to the next candidates. Instead, it reports `chain_exhausted` at the point of failure, even though there are more models remaining in the configured fallback list.

## Environment

- **OpenClaw Version:** 2026.5.22 (a374c3a)
- **Runtime:** embedded (all agents configured with `runtime.type: "embedded"`)
- **Primary model:** `bailian/qwen3.6-plus`
- **Configured fallbacks (11 models):**
  ```
  nvidia/z-ai/glm-5.1
  nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6
  bailian/kimi-k2.5
  nvidia/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-flash
  nvidia/minimaxai/minimax-m2.7
  bailian/glm-5
  nvidia/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-pro
  bailian/qwen3.7-max
  bailian/MiniMax-M2.5
  github-copilot/gpt-5.5
  github-copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6
  ```

## Observed Behavior (from gateway logs)

### Event 1 — 2026-05-26 15:04:56 (bailian/qwen3-coder-plus)
```json
{
  "event": "embedded_run_failover_decision",
  "provider": "bailian",
  "model": "qwen3-coder-plus",
  "decision": "surface_error",
  "failoverReason": "timeout",
  "fallbackConfigured": false
}
```
→ `qwen3-coder-plus` (used by subagents) is **not covered** by the default model fallback chain. Timed out and surfaced the error directly.

### Event 2 — 2026-05-26 15:07:41 (nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6)
```json
// embedded layer decides to fallback
{
  "event": "embedded_run_failover_decision",
  "provider": "nvidia",
  "model": "moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
  "decision": "fallback_model",
  "failoverReason": "timeout",
  "fallbackConfigured": true,
  "status": 408,
  "errorPreview": "500 Internal server error: unhashable type: 'dict'"
}

// model-fallback layer shows chain exhausted
{
  "event": "model_fallback_decision",
  "decision": "candidate_failed",
  "candidateModel": "moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
  "attempt": 1,
  "total": 1,
  "reason": "timeout",
  "fallbackStepFinalOutcome": "chain_exhausted",
  "fallbackConfigured": false
}
```

Key observation: **`kimi-k2.6` is candidate #2 in the fallbacks array**, yet the log shows `attempt=1/1` and `chain_exhausted`. The fallback chain did **not** continue to `bailian/kimi-k2.5` (candidate #3) or any of the remaining 9 models.

## Expected Behavior

Per [model-failover.md](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/model-failover), OpenClaw should walk the entire configured fallback chain:
> "If that provider is exhausted with a failover-worthy error, move to the next model candidate."
> "If every candidate fails, throw a FallbackSummaryError"

The chain should only stop when **all candidates have been tried** or a non-failover-worthy error (abort, context overflow, user cancel) occurs.

## Analysis

The logs suggest that when a **fallback candidate itself** fails (as opposed to the primary), the embedded run treats that candidate as a new standalone invocation with its own `modelOverrideSource`, which then has no fallback chain configured (`fallbackConfigured: false`). This breaks the chain walk described in the docs.

This may be specific to `runtime.type: "embedded"` — all agents in my config use embedded runtime, so I cannot verify whether the ACP runtime behaves differently.

## Reproduction

1. Configure `agents.defaults.model.primary` to a provider that is currently unreachable.
2. Configure `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks` with multiple candidates.
3. Trigger an agent turn (embedded runtime).
4. Observe: the first or second fallback candidate fails, but the chain does **not** continue to the remaining candidates.

## Impact

- When the primary model is down (e.g., rate-limited provider), users get a failure instead of a working response from a later fallback.
- The issue is especially impactful for embedded agents with long fallback chains, where intermediate providers (like NVIDIA) may return 500/408 errors.
```
title: Embedded runtime: model fallback chain breaks at intermediate candidates instead of walking to the last entry

- Title: [Feature]: Per-Agent TTS/STT Configuration Overrides for Multi-Language Support
- State: OPEN
- Author: acwilan
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

Add support for agent-specific text-to-speech (TTS) and speech-to-text (STT) configuration overrides, enabling multiple agents in a single OpenClaw instance to use different voices, languages, or providers.

### Problem to solve

Currently, TTS/STT configuration is global under messages.tts and tools.media.audio. All agents share the same voice, language, and provider settings. This prevents:

• Multi-language agents: Cannot have one agent speak English and another Spanish
• Voice differentiation: Cannot give different agents distinct voices/personalities
• Provider optimization: Cannot use different TTS providers for different use cases (e.g., fast vs. quality)

### Proposed solution

Extend the agent configuration schema to support TTS/STT overrides:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": {
      "spanish-assistant": {
        "systemPrompt": "Eres un asistente útil...",
        "tts": {
          "provider": "macvoice",
          "voice": "Monica",  // Spanish voice
          "rate": 0.5
        },
        "stt": {
          "provider": "deepgram",
          "language": "es"
        }
      },
      "english-assistant": {
        "systemPrompt": "You are a helpful assistant...",
        "tts": {
          "provider": "elevenlabs",
          "voice": "Adam"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Implementation notes:

• Overrides merge with global messages.tts config (agent-specific wins)
• Passed to speech providers via SpeechSynthesisRequest.providerOverrides or new agentConfig field
• Optional: Support `` directive tags as runtime override

### Alternatives considered

1. Separate OpenClaw instances: High resource overhead, complex management
2. Model-driven language detection: Unreliable, adds latency, doesn't solve voice differentiation
3. Global config switching: Requires restart, not dynamic per conversation

### Impact

• Users: Enable multi-language households, personalized agents, voice branding
• Plugins: Providers receive richer context; no breaking changes
• Ecosystem: Differentiation feature for OpenClaw vs. competitors

### Evidence/examples

• Discord communities: Spanish + English channels need different agent personalities
• Customer service: Escalation bot (professional voice) vs. casual helper (friendly voice)
• Accessibility: Different voices help users distinguish agent roles

### Additional information

• Related: macvoice plugin supports voice selection; lacks agent context
• Similar features: Agent-specific model already exists—pattern exists
• Priority: Medium—workaround exists (model directives) but is clunky
```

- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, impact:session-state, impact:security, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
[Feature]: Per-DM/per-chat default ACP binding for auto-spawning ACP sessions on new topics

## Problem

When using Telegram DM forum topics with ACP agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.), every new topic requires manually running `/acp spawn claude` or asking the main agent to spawn an ACP session. There is no way to configure a DM chat so that new topics automatically bind to an ACP agent.

## Proposed solution

Add a `defaultAcp` config option to `TelegramDirectConfig` (and potentially `TelegramGroupConfig`) that auto-spawns an ACP session when a new topic/thread is created.

### Config example

```json5
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "direct": {
        "63448508": {
          "defaultAcp": {
            "agent": "claude",
            "mode": "persistent",
            "cwd": "/workspace/default"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Behavior

1. User creates a new topic in Telegram DM with the bot
2. On first message in the new topic, OpenClaw detects no existing ACP binding
3. OpenClaw auto-spawns an ACP session using `defaultAcp` config
4. The topic is bound to that ACP session
5. All subsequent messages in the topic route directly to the ACP agent
6. Topics with explicit per-topic config (`topics.<id>.agentId`) should override `defaultAcp`

### Scope

- `TelegramDirectConfig.defaultAcp` - for DM topics
- `TelegramGroupConfig.defaultAcp` - for group forum topics
- Could also be a top-level `TelegramAccountConfig.defaultAcp` for account-wide default

### Alternatives considered

- **Static bindings per topic:** Works but requires manual config for each topic ID upfront
- **Main agent with auto-spawn system prompt:** Wastes a main agent turn just to spawn ACP
- **`/acp spawn` in every new topic:** Manual friction, defeats the purpose of topics as lightweight sessions

### Related

- Thread bindings: `threadBindings.spawnAcpSessions`
- Existing ACP binding model: `bindings[].type="acp"`
- Per-topic agentId routing: `topics.<id>.agentId`

```
